feed.title: Notes from the Rack
feed.subtitle: Reliability, homelab tinkering, and the occasional ride report

entry.title: A quieter pager is a healthier team
entry.summary: Cutting false alarms in half did more for morale than any new tool. The trick was deleting alerts that never led to action, then tightening the ones that remained.
entry.content: We spent a week reading every alert that fired in the last quarter and asking one question for each: did a human do something because of it. If the answer was no, the alert went away. What remained got clearer thresholds and a linked runbook. The on call week is calmer now, and the people who carry it are sleeping again.

entry.title: Backup restore drills, not backup jobs
entry.summary: A backup you have never restored is a hope, not a plan. I now test a full restore on the home cluster on the first weekend of every month.
entry.content: The home lab runs nightly snapshots, but the number that matters is how long a restore takes when something is actually broken. This month it was nineteen minutes from a dead volume to a working service. I write that number down each time so I can watch it drift.

entry.title: Winter base miles in the cold and dark
entry.summary: The trainer is not the enemy. On a wet morning it is the difference between a session and an excuse.
entry.content: December is base season. Low intensity, long and steady, with the feet stretched before the first pedal stroke. When the trail is slick I move the session indoors and call it a win. Consistency beats heroics over a whole winter.

entry.title: The post review that fixed a system, not a person
entry.summary: A config change slipped out without a second set of eyes. The fix was a guardrail, not a reprimand.
entry.content: When the gateway took longer than it should to settle, the easy story was to blame whoever pushed the change. The better story was that nothing stopped a risky change from going out alone. We added a required review on that path and moved on. Blameless is not soft, it is just accurate.
